targets.boundary: Global operations within Microsoft’s carbon inventory
targets.notes: Microsoft plans to achieve this goal by reducing Scope 3 emissions (market-based and management’s criteria) by more than half and by reducing Scope 1 and 2 (market-based) emissions to near zero by the middle of the decade through energy efficiency work and reaching 100% renewable energy by 2025; this carbon inventory is what is in scope of the carbon negative commitment.
targets.title: Carbon negative by 2030

targets.boundary: Global Scope 1, Scope 2 market-based, and Scope 3 business air travel emissions
targets.notes: Microsoft defines carbon neutrality as matching the emissions within the carbon neutrality boundary with an equivalent amount of carbon offsets; starting in FY23, values for Scope 3 business air travel emissions follow management’s criteria as reported under Category 6 – Business Travel with SAFc; values reflect Microsoft’s carbon neutrality at the time of reporting.
targets.title: Carbon neutral every year since fiscal year 2013

targets.boundary: All carbon the company has emitted either directly or by electricity consumption since 1975
targets.notes: By 2050 Microsoft expects to have removed from the environment all the carbon the company has emitted either directly or by electrical consumption since it was founded in 1975; this is described as part of Microsoft’s carbon negative targets.
targets.title: Remove all historical emissions by 2050
